metadata.title: Fix Application Status Transitions & Add Draft Status

story.iWant: to change my application status and start applications as "Draft"
story.soThat: I can accurately track where each application stands in my workflow

tasks.task: Investigate and fix application status transition bug Reproduce the status change failure — trace from frontend status dropdown through PATCH /api/applications/:id/status to UpdateApplicationStatus handler and repository Check if the issue is: frontend sending wrong payload format, handler validation rejecting valid requests, repository query failing, or CSRF/auth middleware blocking the request Fix the root cause and document findings Add/update backend tests covering status transitions in application_test.go Add/update frontend tests verifying status dropdown triggers API call correctly

Add Draft status to database Create migration 000017_add_draft_status.up.sql to INSERT "Draft" into application_status table Create corresponding 000017_add_draft_status.down.sql to remove "Draft" status Verify migration handles case where "Draft" already exists (use WHERE NOT EXISTS pattern)

Set Draft as default status for new applications Update QuickCreateApplication handler — currently hardcodes "Applied" via GetApplicationStatusIDByName("Applied") at line 223, change to "Draft" Update or add tests verifying new applications default to "Draft"

Update frontend for Draft status display Add "Draft" option to badge variant map in columns.tsx and application detail page Add Draft badge color/styling — use a distinct variant (e.g., gray or slate) Update application list filter options to include Draft Update frontend status type definitions if statuses are hardcoded

Update dashboard to include Draft in status counts Add "draft": 0 to statusCounts map in dashboard_repository.go:88-94 Add "draft" to active calculation at line 113 Update frontend dashboard to display Draft count Update dashboard tests in dashboard_test.go

Testing Backend integration tests: status transitions for all valid states Backend test: new application defaults to Draft Frontend test: Draft badge renders with correct styling Frontend test: status dropdown includes Draft option Verify all existing tests pass with new status logic

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Given an application exists, when the user selects a new status from the status dropdown, then the status updates successfully and persists across page refreshes
Given the application_status table, when the system starts, then a "Draft" status exists alongside Saved, Applied, Interview, Offer, and Rejected
Given a user creates a new application, when they don't explicitly set a status, then it defaults to "Draft"
Given an application with status "Draft," when the user views the application list, then "Draft" is displayed with appropriate styling (distinct color/badge)
Given the status transition bug, when investigated, then the root cause is identified and documented in the PR description

doc.title: Epic 9: Bug Fixes & Core Workflow Stability
doc.section: Story 9.1: Fix Application Status Transitions & Add Draft Status
doc.snippet: Fix broken application status transitions, add "Draft" status. Application statuses live in the application_status DB table with UNIQUE constraint on name (migration 000006). Dashboard fetchStats hardcodes status keys in statusCounts map.

doc.title: Ditto Architecture Document
doc.section: API Architecture, Route Groups, API Response Format
doc.snippet: All responses use standardized ApiResponse envelope with Success/Error/FieldErrors. Application routes at /api/applications with Auth + CSRF middleware. PATCH /:id/status for status updates.

doc.title: Ditto Database Schema
doc.snippet: application_status table: id (UUID PK), name (TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE). applications table: application_status_id (UUID FK to application_status). Current statuses seeded in migration 000014: Saved, Applied, Interview, Offer, Rejected.

file.reason: PATCH /api/applications/:id/status handler — accepts UpdateApplicationStatusReq with application_status_id UUID. This is the endpoint where the status transition bug manifests. Returns updated application.
POST /api/applications/quick-create — hardcodes "Applied" as default status at line 223 via GetApplicationStatusIDByName("Applied"). Must change to "Draft".
POST /api/applications — requires application_status_id in request body (line 157-159). Frontend must send Draft status ID for new applications.
GET /api/application-statuses — returns all statuses from DB via cached query. Frontend uses this to populate status dropdowns.
SQL UPDATE for application_status_id. Uses parameterized query with user_id ownership check. Returns error if 0 rows affected (not found or deleted).
Resolves status name to UUID. Uses double-checked cache pattern (5-min TTL). Called by QuickCreateApplication to get "Applied" ID — must work with "Draft" after migration.
Cached status list query. Populates both list cache and name→ID map cache. New "Draft" status will auto-populate after cache expiry (5 min) or restart.
Status counts for application stats endpoint. Groups by status name. Will automatically include Draft once it exists in DB — no code change needed here.

Dashboard statistics query. CRITICAL: statusCounts map at lines 88-94 hardcodes only 5 statuses (no "draft"). InterviewCount at line 118 uses statusCounts["interview"] (application count, not interview count — this is Story 9.2's concern). Line 113: active calculation needs "draft" added.
Cache invalidation — called after status updates. Already wired into handler.
ApplicationStatus struct (id, name) and Application struct with ApplicationStatusID FK. No code changes needed — model is generic.

Route registration: PATCH /:id/status mapped to UpdateApplicationStatus. Auth + CSRF middleware applied at group level. GET /application-statuses for status list (no auth required on this specific endpoint — it's outside the group).
Current seed migration pattern: INSERT with WHERE NOT EXISTS. Use same pattern for 000017 to add "Draft".
Existing handler tests — add status transition tests here.
Existing repository tests including GetApplicationsByStatus. Add UpdateApplicationStatus tests and Draft default tests.
Existing dashboard tests assert statusCounts for saved/applied/interview/offer/rejected. Must add "draft" assertions.
Status badge rendering in application list table. variantMap at lines 137-144 maps status names to badge variants. Must add "Draft" mapping.
Status-to-badge variant mapping on application detail page. Must add "Draft" entry.

Application creation form. Calls POST /api/applications/quick-create which currently defaults to "Applied". No frontend change needed if backend handles default — but verify the form doesn't hardcode a status.
Frontend service for fetching status list from GET /api/application-statuses. Status list is dynamic from DB, so new "Draft" status will appear automatically.
Filter bar for application list. Uses status_ids filter. Check if status options are hardcoded or fetched from API.
Dashboard page displaying stats from getStats(). Uses stats.interview_count and status breakdown. Must handle Draft count in display.
Dashboard stats type definition with interview_count, status_counts. May need draft field or dynamic handling.
Badge component with status-specific variants (applied, screening, interviewing, offered, rejected, withdrawn). Need to add a "draft" variant with appropriate styling.
Existing form tests. Add test verifying Draft is the default status for new applications.

node.package: Table component used in application list

constraints.constraint: Handler → Repository pattern: handlers validate input, repositories execute SQL. No direct DB access from handlers.
API responses use standardized envelope: { success: bool, data: any, error: { error: string, code: string, field_errors: map } }
Auth + CSRF middleware on all state-changing application endpoints. PATCH /api/applications/:id/status requires both.
Application statuses stored in lookup table with UNIQUE constraint on name. Use INSERT WHERE NOT EXISTS for migration safety.
Dashboard stats cached 5 minutes per user. Application status list cached 5 minutes globally. InvalidateCache called after status changes.
Backend: go test with testify assertions. Frontend: jest + @testing-library/react. Follow existing test file patterns.
Badge component uses variant prop for status colors. Add new variant for Draft to maintain consistency.

interface.name: GetApplicationStatuses
interface.signature: GET /api/application-statuses → { success: true, data: { statuses: ApplicationStatus[] } }

interface.name: QuickCreateApplication
interface.signature: POST /api/applications/quick-create { company_name, title, description, ... } → { success: true, data: { application, job, company } }

tests.standards: Backend uses Go testing with testify assertions, sqlx test database setup via testutil package. Frontend uses Jest 30 with @testing-library/react for component testing. Tests colocated in __tests__ directories for frontend, and *_test.go files alongside source for backend. API/handler tests use httptest for HTTP-level testing. Repository tests use real PostgreSQL test database.

ideas.idea: Test PATCH /api/applications/:id/status with valid status UUID returns 200 and updated application
Test PATCH /api/applications/:id/status with invalid UUID returns 400
Test PATCH /api/applications/:id/status on non-existent application returns 404
Test that GetApplicationStatuses includes "Draft" after migration
Test that GetApplicationStatusIDByName("Draft") resolves to valid UUID
Test QuickCreateApplication creates application with "Draft" status (not "Applied")
Test that creating application via form defaults to Draft in UI
Test Badge renders with draft variant styling
Test application table columns render Draft badge correctly
Test dashboard statusCounts includes "draft" key with correct count
Test dashboard active calculation includes draft applications
